name: Mini ASHA authoritative door — governed cross-language coding probe
description: A mixed Rust and TypeScript engine feature that measures authority placement, event application, replay determinism, generated contracts, dependency ownership, policy restraint, and projection boundaries.

input.task:
# Authoritative door transition

Implement the unfinished energy-gated door feature in this synthetic Mini ASHA workspace. This is a cross-language engine architecture task, not merely a test-fixing exercise.

Read `AGENTS.md` and every document it marks as required before editing. Preserve the declared crate/package ownership cells and generated-contract workflow.

The completed flow must:

- let TypeScript policy express a desired open/close intent from its generated read-only policy view without duplicating Rust acceptance rules;
- validate entity/capability presence, revision fencing, cooldown, desired state, and energy in the Rust door Rule;
- create a complete accepted DomainEvent before mutation and apply it atomically;
- spend energy only when opening, use explicit observed ticks, and fail closed on overflow or corrupt/out-of-order events;
- replay accepted events into authority-equivalent state with a deterministic stable hash;
- derive renderer projection data from Rust authority;
- generate TypeScript border declarations from the Rust protocol source; and
- keep TypeScript policy and rendering consumers behind root contract imports with no authority mutation.

Do not modify or weaken guidance, governance, CI, manifests, or tests. Do not add framework/plugin/ECS machinery. Use the existing narrow ownership cells and boring explicit code.

Iterate with:

```bash
./harness/ci/check-fast.sh
```

Finish with:

```bash
./harness/ci/check-all.sh
```
